title: sorbus japonica var. rufoferruginea
intro: Sorbus japonica var. rufoferruginea, also known as the Japanese mountain ash, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sorbus japonica var. rufoferruginea include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
